title: ugget
html:
ugget

  • Last UpdatedDez 30, 2022
    • Uniface
    • 10.4

Move data from the current occurrence of the specified entity or current entity into a program buffer. 

short ugget (unsigned char*entity, unsigned char*buffer, shortmax);

Parameters

entity
The name of the entity whose data to get. A value of NULL denotes the current entity.
buffer
(Output) The buffer containing the data.
max
The maximum number of bytes that should be moved. This should not exceed the size of the buffer.

Return Values

Table 1. Values returned by ugget and uggetc
Value Meaning
>0 The number of bytes copied into the buffer.
0 An error occurred.
<0 The negated number of bytes copied into the buffer. The negative status indicates that more data is available in the field, and that you should call uggetc to get additional data.

Use

Library: yrtl

Description

ugget moves the data in the current occurrence of entity to buffer. To use the current entity, specify NULL as the entity name. If there is more data in the field than the buffer can hold, ugget returns a negative status. In this case, you must call uggetc repeatedly to get additional data, until uggetc returns a positive status.

Before copying the data to your buffer, it is converted from internal Uniface format to external DBMS format. This means that the layout of your buffer depends on which DBMS your entity is assigned to, and possibly also on specific settings for the database driver.

To see how the data will be packed in the buffer, set /pri=255 and look in the message frame while retrieving data from your entity, and search for the hexadecimal I/O buffer dump for your entity, for example:

0000: 36 20 20 36 20 20 20 20 20 20 20 20 20 20 20 20: 6 6
0016: 20 20 20 20 20 20 20 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00: .........

This example is for a simple entity with an SC3 and an SC20 field. Depending on the database, the layout may be slightly different.

Note: When using this function, it is important to know which fields of the entity are painted on your component, and what the setting is of the field list for the entity. Fields that are excluded from the field list will not be included in your buffer (their slots in the buffer will be filled with binary zeroes). To make sure that ugget gives you all the data, we recommend that you set the field list for your component entity to 'All fields'.

Profile and subfield separator characters, if present in a field, are also returned in the buffer, so you should be prepared to handle these. For information about the representation of these characters, see the "Subfield Separator and Profile Characters" topic.

Example: ugget and uggetc

The following example illustrates how to use ugget and uggetc to get the data from the current occurrence into a buffer. Because the size of the occurrence is unknown, we read fixed chunks and expand the buffer as necessary. The choice of 100 as the size of the fixed chunks to read is arbitrary.

#include <h3gl.h>
#define CHUNKSIZE 100

EXPORT void getoccurrence ()
{
char* buf;
int len;
int totlen;

buf = malloc(CHUNKSIZE); /* Allocate the buffer */

len = ugget(NULL, buf, CHUNKSIZE);/* Get the first chunk of data */
totlen = abs(len); /* Set total length */

while ( len < 0 ) /* More to come */
{
/* Make room for next chunk */
buf = realloc(buf, totlen+CHUNKSIZE);
/* Read next chunk */
len = uggetc(NULL, buf+totlen, CHUNKSIZE);
/* Update total length */
totlen += abs(len);
}

buf = realloc(buf, totlen+1); /* Make room for terminator */
buf[totlen] = 0; /* Add terminator */
}
Related Links
  • Subfield Separator and Profile Characters

    Uniface uses part of the reserved area of each meta character set (that is, the first 32 characters plus the last character) to store characters with special meaning within Uniface, such as subfield separators and profile characters.

  • uggetc

    Move additional data from the current occurrence of the specified entity or current entity into a program buffer.
